title: Constant Contact vs Sendinblue (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Constant Contact and Sendinblue email marketing tools. See pricing, features, ratings, and which is best for your business.
h1: Constant Contact vs Sendinblue: Complete Comparison
intro: Constant Contact and Sendinblue (now Brevo) are two popular email marketing platforms with different strengths and pricing models. Constant Contact excels for nonprofits and local businesses seeking simplicity, while Sendinblue offers a more robust all-in-one CRM suite with a generous free tier. Your choice depends on budget, feature needs, and whether you require transactional email capabilities.
choose_a_if: You're a nonprofit or local business wanting simplicity and dedicated event management tools
You need strong e-commerce integration and social media ad capabilities
You prefer established platforms (founded 1995) with a longer track record
choose_b_if: You want a free tier supporting up to 100k subscribers with 300 daily emails
You need an all-in-one CRM solution with SMS, WhatsApp, chat, and transactional API
You prioritize higher customer satisfaction ratings and modern feature sets

faq.q: Is Constant Contact better than Sendinblue?
faq.a: Neither is universally 'better'—it depends on your needs. Sendinblue has superior ratings (4.5 vs 4.0 on G2, 4.3 vs 2.8 on Trustpilot) and offers more comprehensive CRM features. However, Constant Contact is better suited for nonprofits and local businesses that need straightforward email marketing with event management and social media ad tools.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Constant Contact or Sendinblue?
faq.a: Sendinblue is significantly cheaper for budget-conscious users since it offers a free tier with 100k subscriber support and 300 daily emails at no cost. Constant Contact has no free option and starts at $12/month. However, Sendinblue's paid plans begin at $25/month, so Constant Contact is cheaper once you need paid features.

faq.q: Can I switch from Sendinblue to Constant Contact?
faq.a: Yes, you can switch between platforms. Most email marketing services support list exports in standard formats (CSV). You'll need to import your contacts and recreate your campaigns in Constant Contact, though the process is straightforward for most users. Check both platforms' migration guides for detailed instructions.
